briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 3, 2026.

sections.title: 🌱 Climate Adaptation and Agricultural Innovation
sections.content: While climate change is altering the agricultural production landscape, some regions are seizing this as a new opportunity. Farmers in the Gyeongnam region are actively cultivating high-income crops like mangoes, turning the climate change crisis into an opportunity to increase their income [1]. Furthermore, in response to the collapsing production map due to the climate crisis, Dae-a Cheonggwa and Hannongyeon are attempting a breakthrough through win-win cooperation, drawing attention to proactive climate adaptation efforts in the agricultural sector [4]. These examples demonstrate positive movements to overcome the challenges posed by climate change and explore new avenues.

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Various local governments across the country are making multifaceted efforts to achieve carbon neutrality goals. Wonju City is pursuing comprehensive policies, including its 2026 carbon neutrality target, across welfare, agriculture, and other sectors, aiming for a sustainable city [7]. Daejeon Daedeok-gu will also operate family-participatory carbon neutrality programs throughout May to raise citizens' environmental awareness and encourage practical action [8]. Additionally, educational programs for practicing carbon neutrality in daily life are actively underway, such as experiential workshops at the Wonju Climate Change Promotion Center where participants recycle coffee grounds to make plant pots [3].

sections.title: 🌱 Commercialization of Renewable Energy and Eco-Friendly Technologies
sections.content: The expansion of renewable energy and the development of eco-friendly technologies are becoming key drivers in responding to climate change. Recently, the scope of renewable energy is expanding as standards for recognizing air heat as renewable energy have been established and requirements for heat pump application have been specified [9]. Korea Midland Power is maximizing efficiency by transitioning its renewable energy operating system to an AI-based integrated management approach [10]. Furthermore, HD Hyundai Electric plans to supply its domestically developed eco-friendly high-voltage circuit breakers, the first of their kind in Korea, to a substation in Sweden, demonstrating the potential for international commercialization of domestic eco-friendly technologies [12]. These technological advancements will contribute to building a sustainable energy system.

sections.title: ⚠️ Direct Threats of Climate Change and Health Issues
sections.content: Climate change poses a direct threat to our daily lives and health, necessitating urgent proactive responses. With heatwaves tending to arrive earlier due to climate change, local governments in the Ulsan region are taking preemptive action [5]. Notably, reports indicating that climate change inflicts greater damage when treated as a health crisis suggest that it is not merely an environmental issue but a serious public health challenge [21]. Swift and systematic policy development is required to effectively address these threats.

sections.title: 🌍 Societal Discourse and International Cooperation in the Era of Climate Crisis
sections.content: The era of climate crisis demands extensive societal discourse and international cooperation. As achieving 'carbon neutrality' is recognized as a realistically challenging task, the need for more meticulous strategy development to realize this goal is being raised [6]. Furthermore, discussions on the role of insurance in the age of climate crisis are taking place through publications, indicating active contemplation on changes in financial and social systems [2]. Internationally, the IAF Global Space Climate Change Conference is scheduled to be held in Kigali in 2026 [20], and local artists are addressing climate change issues in new residency programs [22], demonstrating ongoing efforts across various sectors to raise awareness and explore response measures for climate change.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted various domestic and international efforts to address climate change and the challenges we face. Continued attention and action are crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: HD Hyundai Electric Completes Approval for Eco-Friendly High-Voltage Circuit Breaker… 'To Be Supplied to Swedish Substations'
articles.original_title: HD현대일렉트릭, 친환경 고압차단기 승인 완료…“스웨덴 변전소에 공급”
articles.description: HD Hyundai Electric announced on the 3rd that its 145kV (kilovolt) 'SF6-free high-voltage circuit breaker,' developed for the first time in Korea, has completed final approval testing and will be supplied to substations operated by a Swedish power company. The SF6-free high-voltage circuit breaker is classified as an eco-friendly power device that does not use SF6, a representative fluorinated greenhouse gas. SF6 has been widely used in high-voltage power equipment due to its excellent insulation and interruption performance, but it contributes to global warming...

summary.phenomenon: HD Hyundai Electric's domestically developed first-ever 145kV SF6-free high-voltage circuit breaker has completed final approval testing and is scheduled to be supplied to substations operated by a Swedish power company on the 3rd.
summary.cause: This high-voltage circuit breaker was developed to avoid using SF6, a representative fluorinated greenhouse gas. SF6 has been widely used in high-voltage power equipment due to its excellent insulation and interruption performance, but it is a substance that contributes to global warming.

articles.description: In a previous column titled 'Forgotten Native Plants: Reasons to Bring Them Back to Our Tables,' we introduced the Korean mock orange (Philadelphus schrenkii). The Korean mock orange was not merely a rediscovery of an edible resource but also an example demonstrating that our native plants possess industrial potential. However, there are still many underutilized native plants in Korea that have not undergone sufficient scientific verification and utilization. Climate change, global supply chain instability, food and resource...

summary.phenomenon: In a previous column, the Korean mock orange was introduced as a native plant with edible and industrial potential, but there are still many underutilized native plants in Korea lacking sufficient scientific verification and application.
